title: feat: store discord bot secrets in sops
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
Moves the Discord bot secrets out of plaintext `.env` (previously only on the EC2 host) into sops-encrypted files committed to the repo, following the existing `operation/kpi` convention.

- Adds `secrets/env.json` (sops-encrypted, 3 age recipients) for `discord-bot-family`, `catgpt-bot`, `opposite-prompt-bot` — holds the `BOT_TOKEN_*` Discord tokens + `TEXT_POLLINATIONS_TOKEN`
- Adds `decrypt-vars` npm script per bot: `sops decrypt secrets/env.json --output-type dotenv > .env`
- `catgpt-bot`: adds missing `.env.example`
- `discord-bot-family`: documents the sops flow in README, adds `POLLINATIONS_API_URL` to `.env.example`, fixes stale `start-bots.sh` → `start-all.sh`

Decrypted `.env` stays gitignored; encrypted `secrets/env.json` is committed. Verified each file round-trips (`sops decrypt` reproduces the original `.env`) and contains only `ENC[...]` values (no plaintext).

No behavior change to the running bots — start scripts still read `.env`; this only changes where secrets are sourced from. On the host, run `npm run decrypt-vars` to regenerate `.env`.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: Locking the vault
gist.blurb: We've upgraded how we store our Discord bot secrets, moving them into encrypted vaults. Better security means our bots can keep buzzing safely!
gist.summary: Moved Discord bot tokens for catgpt-bot, opposite-prompt-bot, and discord-bot-family from plaintext .env files to sops-encrypted secrets/env.json files.
gist.impact: Developers can now securely manage and sync bot credentials via the repository using the new `npm run decrypt-vars` script instead of manual server configuration.
gist.visual_concept: The pollinations bee wearing a tiny security guard hat or holding a glowing padlock, standing next to a chunky metal safe or vault covered in vines and flowers. The vault represents the encrypted sops secrets.
